title: [lichen_l2.c:70] lichen_schc_init never called - SCHC module uninitialized
description:
Final pass: This was missed because reviewers focused on link_ctx and replay_table initialization, not SCHC.

Root cause: The code comment at line 70 lists lichen_schc_init() as a required symbol from schc.h, but this function is never called anywhere in the codebase. The SCHC compression/decompression used by lichen_link_tx/rx relies on the SCHC state being initialized.

Searching for calls:
- grep -rn 'lichen_schc_init' subsys/lichen/*.c  (no hits outside comments)
- lichen_l2_iface_init() calls lichen_link_init() and lichen_replay_table_init() but not lichen_schc_init()

Impact: If SCHC requires initialization state (e.g., compiled rules, context), decompression may fail silently or use uninitialized data. Need to verify whether lichen_schc_init() exists and what state it initializes.

Fix: Either call lichen_schc_init() in iface_init path, or document if SCHC is stateless and the comment is stale.
close_reason: Fixed: Updated comment at line 70 - lichen_schc_init() does not exist; SCHC module is stateless (compress/decompress are pure functions). Comment now accurately lists lichen_schc_compress() and lichen_schc_decompress().
